week_summary: Today was a tale of two markets — big-name tech stocks like OKTA (+27.5%), NTAP (+26.7%), PLTR (+9.1%), and NOW (+13.6%) absolutely exploded on earnings beats and AI momentum, while small-cap stocks got hit hard (Russell 2000 down 0.5%) and GAP cratered 16.7% on bad news. The Dow gained 300 points led by IBM and Salesforce surging. Volatility (VIX) dropped 2.3% which means the market is calming down, but only 2 out of 11 market sectors are actually moving higher — so this is very much a stock-picker's market right now, not a rising tide. Biggest drama of the day: AST SpaceMobile dropped 17% after a Blue Origin rocket exploded, while Virgin Galactic somehow jumped 11% on the same news.
market_mood: Markets are mixed — ROK is watching closely for clear signals
rok_message: My top pick right now is PLTR (Palantir) — it jumped 9% today and the government AI spending wave that drives their business is only getting bigger, with analysts seeing another 17% upside from here. But the most important thing happening this week is Broadcom earnings — if AVGO beats, every AI stock you own goes up; if they miss, even Nvidia and Palantir will take a hit so be ready. Biggest thing to avoid right now: GAP is in freefall after dropping 16.7% today and consumer spending is clearly weakening — stay away from retail stocks. One more thing — the cybersecurity sector (OKTA was up 27.5% today) is the hottest trade in the market right now with CRWD and PANW both reporting this week.

buy_signals.catalyst: Palantir is THE government AI software company — they build the AI systems that the US military, intelligence agencies, and now big corporations use to make decisions. The US government is pouring money into AI defense spending and Palantir wins almost every big contract.
buy_signals.technical_setup: Stock is up 9.1% today on strong volume, building serious upward momentum going into what looks like continued AI government spending
reasons: Stock jumped 9.1% today on above-average volume — big money is clearly rushing in right now
Analysts have a price target of $183.73, which is 17.5% higher than today's price — and many expect that target to be raised after today's move
The entire AI software sector is on fire today — OKTA up 27.5%, NOW up 13.6% — Palantir runs in the same lane and benefits from the same tailwind
Social sentiment is positive and the AI trend driving this stock is only getting stronger as government AI contracts keep rolling in
buy_signals.rok_take: Palantir is the pick of the day in my book — it's up 9% today and the government AI spending wave is just getting started. Analysts see another 17% from here and I think that target goes higher.

buy_signals.catalyst: Okta just reported earnings and the stock exploded 27.5% in a single day — that kind of move tells you the company massively beat what Wall Street expected. Cybersecurity spending is booming as companies scramble to protect their AI systems.
buy_signals.technical_setup: Stock just broke out hard on 2.3x normal trading volume — when a stock jumps this much on heavy volume, big institutions are piling in fast
reasons: Up 27.5% today on 2.3x normal trading volume — this is a massive earnings beat reaction and institutions are buying hard
Cybersecurity is one of the hottest sectors right now — every company upgrading to AI also needs to secure that AI, which means more Okta contracts
The broader software earnings wave is strong today — NTAP up 26.7%, NOW up 13.6% — confirms the sector has real momentum and Okta is riding it
Despite the huge pop, there's still room to run as the cybersecurity industry keeps growing
buy_signals.rok_take: Okta just had one of its best single days in years — a 27.5% jump means the earnings were seriously impressive. The note of caution: the stock has now run past analyst targets, so consider buying a smaller position and watching for a brief pullback to get a better entry.

buy_signals.catalyst: ServiceNow builds the software that companies use to automate their IT departments and business operations — and AI is making their product dramatically more powerful and in-demand. They just jumped 13.6% today and analysts see another 15% upside from here.
buy_signals.technical_setup: Up 13.6% today on 1.3x normal volume with analyst strong buy rating — the stock is breaking out of a range on meaningful buying pressure
reasons: Jumped 13.6% today and analysts rate it a strong buy with a $142.50 price target — that's 15% more upside even after today's big move
ServiceNow's AI-powered automation tools are becoming must-haves for large companies — every Fortune 500 company is trying to cut costs with AI and ServiceNow is a top solution
The entire enterprise software sector is surging today alongside Palantir, OKTA, and NTAP — this is a real sector rotation into software happening in real-time
Dow rose 300 points today led by Salesforce (same industry) — the tailwind is real and broad
buy_signals.rok_take: ServiceNow is the kind of boring-but-brilliant company that quietly makes a ton of money helping big businesses run smoother. Up 13% today and analysts still see 15% more upside — that's a strong combination.

buy_signals.catalyst: Nvidia makes the chips that power literally every major AI application in the world — ChatGPT, Google AI, Microsoft Copilot, all of it runs on Nvidia hardware. Demand is so high that customers are waiting in line for chips and paying premium prices.
buy_signals.technical_setup: Trading at normal volume levels today which actually means big hands aren't selling — stock is consolidating before what could be a next leg up
reasons: Analysts have a strong buy rating with a price target of $296.81 — that's 37% higher than today's price of $216
AVGO (Broadcom) reports earnings this week in the same AI chip space — if they beat, the whole sector including Nvidia gets a boost
The AI chip demand story keeps accelerating — every tech company on earth is building out AI infrastructure and they all need Nvidia chips to do it
Only 1.3% of shares are being bet against (shorted) — almost nobody thinks this stock goes down, which is rare and tells you something
buy_signals.rok_take: Nvidia is still my long-term top pick. Yes it's already run a lot, but analysts see 37% more upside and the AI buildout has years to go. The upcoming Broadcom earnings this week will be a key signal for the whole AI trade.

buy_signals.catalyst: SoFi is a digital bank that's been growing fast — up 7.9% today on above-normal volume. They offer everything from personal loans to investing to banking, and young people are switching to them from traditional banks at a steady pace.
buy_signals.technical_setup: Up 7.9% today on 1.4x normal trading volume — buyers clearly stepped in today with conviction
reasons: Up 7.9% today on above-average volume of 1.4x normal — that's not a coincidence, something is driving this buying
Analysts see the stock reaching $21 from today's $18.32 — that's about 14.5% more upside from here
About 13.7% of shares are being bet against the stock right now — if good news keeps coming, those short-sellers will be forced to buy, which pushes the price up even faster
The broader financial sector got a boost today with the Dow up 300 points, and digital banking continues to take market share from traditional banks
buy_signals.rok_take: SoFi had a strong day and has room to run to $21 according to analysts. The 13.7% short interest is a bonus — if the stock keeps climbing, those short sellers get squeezed and the price can spike fast.

buy_signals.catalyst: Ameriprise is one of America's biggest financial planning companies — they manage wealth for millions of Americans. Stock is trading at a cheap price relative to their earnings (low valuation) and analysts see 21.6% upside from here.
buy_signals.technical_setup: Up 1.1% today while the broader market is mixed — showing quiet strength and holding well while others drop
reasons: Analysts have a buy rating with a $540.82 price target — that's 21.6% upside from today's price of $444
Trading at just 11x earnings which is very cheap for a profitable financial company of this size — this is the kind of undervalued stock that big funds quietly accumulate
Only 5.4% of shares are being bet against — low skepticism from professionals means the bull case is broadly accepted
Social sentiment is positive and there's been meaningful social chatter (34 posts) around this ticker — awareness is growing
buy_signals.rok_take: Ameriprise is the steady, boring winner in this list — not flashy but analysts see 21% upside and the valuation is genuinely cheap. This is the kind of stock you buy and let sit.

buy_signals.catalyst: Broadcom reports earnings this week and they make the custom AI chips that companies like Google and Meta use to build their AI systems — this is one of the most important earnings reports of the season for the entire AI trade.
buy_signals.technical_setup: The AI chip sector is on fire today with multiple related stocks jumping — if peers continue beating, Broadcom enters earnings with serious momentum behind it
buy_signals.options_play: Consider buying call options expiring after the earnings date — if Broadcom beats like others in this space have, the stock could jump 10-20% overnight
reasons: Earnings report coming this week after market close — this is a must-watch event that could move the entire AI sector
The AI chip and infrastructure sector has been printing massive earnings beats all season — NTAP up 26.7% today, Palantir up 9.1% — Broadcom plays in the same space
Broadcom makes custom AI silicon for the biggest tech companies on earth — demand is enormous and growing, which sets up a strong earnings beat
The AI software sector's huge moves today (OKTA +27.5%, NOW +13.6%) confirm the broader AI theme is alive and well heading into Broadcom's report
buy_signals.rok_take: Broadcom earnings this week is THE most important event on the calendar right now. The whole AI trade rides on this report — if they beat, expect Nvidia, Palantir, and the whole sector to jump. If they miss, buckle up.

reasons: Stock crashed 16.7% in a single day on 2.4x normal trading volume — when a stock drops this much this fast with this much volume, big investors are running for the exits and the selling usually continues for days

A 47-year-old high-end steak and seafood chain closing 80 locations made headlines today — this signals consumers are pulling back on spending, which is terrible news for a clothing retailer like Gap that depends on discretionary spending
Even though analysts have a buy rating and $30 target, that target will almost certainly be cut after a 16.7% single-day drop — the story has clearly changed
sell_signals.rok_take: When a stock drops 16.7% in one day on heavy selling, that's a fire alarm going off. Get out now and wait for the dust to settle — there's no need to catch a falling knife here.

reasons: Down 15.6% today after a Blue Origin rocket explosion — AST SpaceMobile relies on rocket launches to deploy its satellites, and a high-profile launch failure creates real uncertainty about their launch schedule and costs
The stock was already trading at an astronomical valuation for a company that doesn't make money (negative earnings) — now with operational risk added on top, the downside is significant
Short sellers already had 17.6% of shares bet against this stock before today's crash — the professionals clearly saw risks that are now materializing
sell_signals.rok_take: The rocket explosion is a serious problem for a company that literally depends on rockets to build its business. Down 15% already and the bad news isn't priced in yet — reduce or exit.

reasons: Stock crashed 19.9% today on 3.6x normal trading volume — this is a massive post-earnings sell-off and the damage usually takes weeks to heal
When a stock drops 20% in one day, it almost always means the company reported numbers or gave guidance that was much worse than Wall Street expected — that's a broken story
Even with analysts maintaining a buy rating and $87.92 target, that target will likely be cut — and a 20% drop means institutional investors are exiting in size
sell_signals.rok_take: Down 20% in a single day is brutal and the selling is nowhere near done. Unless you have a very long time horizon and strong conviction, this is a get-out situation.

reasons: Up 15.7% today on the back of a competitor's rocket explosion — this is the worst kind of gain, built on someone else's disaster rather than Virgin Galactic's own good news
Stock is trading on 4.3x its normal volume which means this spike is likely a short-term reaction, not genuine buying based on the company's fundamentals
Analysts have a price target of $3.55 — that's 32% BELOW today's price, meaning even after today's pop, the professionals think this stock is massively overvalued
sell_signals.rok_take: This is a dead cat bounce — the stock spiked because a competitor blew up a rocket, not because Virgin Galactic did anything good. Analysts think it's worth 32% less than today's price. If you're holding, take the gift and sell.

watch_list.why_watching: CrowdStrike reports earnings this week and they're the #1 cybersecurity company in the world. After Okta just jumped 27.5% on its earnings beat today, CrowdStrike could do the same — cybersecurity spending is exploding as companies try to protect their AI systems
watch_list.trigger: Earnings beat Wall Street's $1.07 per share estimate and they raise their forecast for the rest of the year
watch_list.risk: A miss or weak guidance could drop the stock 15-20% fast, especially given how much the sector has already run

watch_list.why_watching: Another major cybersecurity company reporting earnings this week. After Okta's massive 27.5% single-day move on earnings, every cybersecurity stock is in focus. Palo Alto is the biggest of the bunch and a beat could send it significantly higher.
watch_list.trigger: Earnings beat the $0.80 per share estimate and the company's revenue guidance comes in above expectations
watch_list.risk: The stock has already had a big run — if earnings disappoint even slightly, expect a sharp pullback

watch_list.why_watching: Costco dropped 4.7% today on above-average volume, which is unusual for one of the most stable companies in America. This could be a buying opportunity — their membership model is basically recession-proof and analysts see 13.5% upside from here.
watch_list.trigger: Stock stabilizes and stops falling, or next earnings report shows continued membership growth
watch_list.risk: Consumer spending fears are real right now — if the economy slows, even Costco could see fewer big-ticket purchases. The restaurant chain closing 80 locations today is a warning sign for consumer health.

watch_list.why_watching: lululemon reports earnings this week. The stock has been beaten up badly over the past year as growth slowed in North America. But they've been expanding internationally and the question is whether that's working. A good report could spark a major bounce.
watch_list.trigger: Earnings beat the $2.59 per share estimate and management gives positive commentary on international growth
watch_list.risk: North American consumer weakness is a real threat — if domestic same-store sales are down, the stock drops hard regardless of international

watch_list.why_watching: Dollar General reports earnings this week and analysts are warning they might give conservative guidance due to cost pressures. But Dollar General serves lower-income shoppers who are actually spending MORE at discount stores when the economy is tough — so this could surprise to the upside.
watch_list.trigger: Earnings beat $1.49 per share estimate and management commentary about foot traffic shows lower-income consumers holding up
watch_list.risk: Oppenheimer specifically flagged cost headwinds and economic pressure on their customers today — a miss could push the stock down 10-15%

notable_trends: Enterprise software just had one of its best single days of the year — OKTA jumped 27.5%, ServiceNow up 13.6%, NTAP up 26.7%, and Palantir up 9.1% all in one day. Companies are spending heavily on AI-powered software to run their businesses and the earnings are showing it.

The consumer is showing cracks — a 47-year-old restaurant chain closed 80 locations today, Gap dropped 16.7%, and Costco fell nearly 5%. The higher-end consumer is starting to pull back, which could be an early warning sign for retail stocks heading into summer.

Space stocks are having a wild week — AST SpaceMobile down 15.6% and Planet Labs down 8% after a Blue Origin rocket explosion, while Virgin Galactic surged 11% on the same news. The commercial space race is creating huge single-day volatility, making these stocks high-risk, high-reward plays.

Oil prices are falling today on news that the US and Iran may be working toward a deal — this is good news for consumers at the gas pump but could hurt energy stocks. Keep an eye on energy sector stocks if this deal progresses.

Small-cap stocks are getting left behind while big tech soars — the Russell 2000 (small companies) dropped 0.5% today while the Nasdaq (big tech) jumped 0.5%. Money is flowing into proven large-cap winners, not smaller unproven companies. This 'flight to quality' usually means investors are getting pickier.
macro_risks: Broadcom's earnings report this week is a make-or-break moment for the entire AI trade — if they disappoint, every AI-related stock from Nvidia to Palantir could sell off hard in a single day

Consumer spending is showing real weakness — a major restaurant chain closing 80 locations, Gap crashing 16.7%, and Costco slipping are all happening on the same day. If this spreads, retail and consumer stocks could have a rough summer ahead

Oil prices dropping on US-Iran truce prospects sounds good but creates uncertainty — if the deal falls apart or tensions re-escalate, energy prices spike and that hits inflation, which could make interest rates stay higher for longer and hurt all stocks

sector_heat.hottest: Enterprise Technology / AI Software — OKTA up 27.5%, NTAP up 26.7%, ServiceNow up 13.6%, Palantir up 9.1%, HPE up 14.7% all in a single day. This is where all the money is going right now.
sector_heat.coldest: Consumer Retail — GAP down 16.7%, Costco down 4.7%, and a major restaurant chain closing 80 locations all on the same day. Consumers are tightening their belts and it's showing up in earnings and stock prices.

sector_rotation: Big money is aggressively rotating OUT of consumer and retail stocks and INTO enterprise AI software and infrastructure. Today's massive moves in OKTA, NTAP, and ServiceNow while GAP and Costco fell tells you exactly where institutional money is flowing this week.

short_squeeze_alerts.setup: SoFi jumped 7.9% today on real volume and 13.7% of shares are bet against it — if positive news keeps coming from the digital banking space, short sellers will be forced to buy and could turbocharge the stock price higher.

UiPath has a massive 31.8% of its shares being bet against it, which is extremely high — if the company delivers good news or the AI automation theme catches fire, the forced buying from short sellers could cause an explosive move upward.
BigBear.ai has 26.2% of shares shorted and is up 2.9% today on 1.5x normal volume — if AI defense spending news accelerates, this heavily-shorted small AI company could see a violent short squeeze.

earnings_plays.play: Strong beat expected — Broadcom's custom AI chips are in massive demand from Google and Meta, and the entire AI infrastructure sector has been crushing earnings this season
Beat likely — cybersecurity spending is at all-time highs and Okta's massive 27.5% jump today after its own earnings suggests the sector is firing on all cylinders
Beat expected — same cybersecurity tailwind driving Okta is driving Palo Alto, though the stock has already had a big run so the upside reaction may be more muted
Cautious — Oppenheimer warned today about cost headwinds and economic pressure on Dollar General's core customer base; guidance could disappoint even if the quarter itself is okay
Coin flip — North American traffic has been weak but international expansion is growing; a straddle makes sense if you want to play the volatility without picking a direction

technical_breakouts.description: NetApp exploded 26.7% today on 2.2x normal volume after what appears to be a massive earnings beat — this kind of gap-up breakout on heavy volume often leads to continued buying over the following days as more investors pile in after seeing the move

Hewlett Packard Enterprise jumped 14.7% today on 2.1x normal trading volume — a breakout of this magnitude on above-average volume signals institutional accumulation and often continues higher before pulling back

Post Holdings is down 2.2% today and sitting near deeply oversold levels — analysts have a buy rating with a $122.33 price target against today's $94.29 price, which is 29.7% upside. Stock appears to be washed out and may be setting up for a bounce.

Ameriprise Financial has been pulling back and is now showing signs of stabilizing — with a buy rating, 21.6% upside to the analyst target of $540, and low short interest, this looks like a quality stock that got cheaper than it should be
